text:
Jake Paulwants to box for Team USA at the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles, intending to attempt to qualify for the Games.

The YouTuber has forged an incredibly lucrative professional boxing career so far, taking his record to 10-1 since his debut in 2020.

His opponents have not been stellar, with a number of recognisable names from the world of Mixed Martial Arts, but none who have much credibility in boxing.

The only opponent he has faced of a similar age, weight and experience isTommy Fury, who gave the American his only loss, although it was a very tight split decision win for the Brit.

However, despite this limited experience of any quality, Paul fancies his chances of going to LA, competing for his country and is already eyeing up a gold medal.

Speaking on hisBS W/ Jake Paulpodcast, the 27-year-old said: ‘I’m going to enter the 2028Olympicsfor boxing in Los Angeles. I’m sick and tired of waiting around, waiting for another gold for Team USA.

‘Los Angeles,United States. Sick as f***. Gold medal, you’re mine, brother.’

Paul will not be handed a spot on the American team in four year’s time and would have to qualify, but he says he fancies giving it a crack.

Before he can turn his attention to that endeavour, he has his rearranged bout with boxing legend Mike Tyson coming up on 15 November.

The Problem Child and Iron Mike were due to fight on 20 July but the bout was postponed due to illness to the former heavyweight champion.

The 58-year-old is due to step into the ring with the man over 30 years his junior in November, though, despite concerns over the wisdom of the decision.

title: Jake Paul reveals plan to compete for USA at the Olympics | Metro News
description: Jake Paul wants to box for Team USA at the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles, intending to attempt to qualify for the Games.
